lesson.title: Goroutines
lesson.summary: Run functions concurrently — lightweight threads that make Go scale.

sections.heading: What is a goroutine
sections.bodyHtml:
A goroutine is a lightweight thread managed by the Go runtime. Just add go before a function call to run it concurrently:

func say(msg string) {
    for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
        fmt.Println(msg)
        time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
    }
}

go say("hello")  // runs in background
go say("world")  // runs in background
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)

Goroutines share the same address space, so you need to synchronise access to shared data (covered in the channels lesson).

sections.heading: Goroutines are cheap
sections.bodyHtml:
A goroutine starts at ~4 KB of stack, growing as needed. You can start thousands, even hundreds of thousands, on a single machine. Compare that to OS threads which use ~1 MB each:

for i := 0; i < 10000; i++ {
    go func(n int) {
        fmt.Println(n)
    }(i)
}
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)

The go keyword does not wait — execution continues immediately to the next line. The program exits when main returns, even if goroutines are still running.

sections.heading: Anonymous goroutines and closures
sections.bodyHtml:
You can launch an anonymous function as a goroutine. Be careful with loop variables — pass them as arguments:

// BAD — all goroutines see the same 'i'
for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
    go func() { fmt.Println(i) }()
}

// GOOD — each goroutine gets its own copy
for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
    go func(n int) { fmt.Println(n) }(i)
}

sections.heading: Your turn
sections.bodyHtml:
Open practice/goroutines/main.go.

Write a program that launches 5 goroutines, each printing its number (1-5). Use time.Sleep to wait for them to finish.

questions.prompt: When does the program exit if main starts several goroutines?

options.label: When main returns, even if goroutines are still running

questions.prompt: Approximately how much stack does a goroutine start with?

exercises.title: Five goroutines
exercises.instructions: Launch 5 goroutines printing 1-5. Use time.Sleep to let them all finish before main exits.
